asset_details.name: Incentive Tokens
asset_details.description: Whitelisted ERC20 tokens offered by protocols to validators/boosters.

attack_surface.data_description: Berachain network RPC details (URL, Chain ID, etc.) provided by the user.

behavior_details.name: Connect Wallet
behavior_details.description: User connects their wallet to interact with dApps/network

data_flow_details.description: User delegates BGT, votes, or submits governance proposal

boundary_details.name: Governance Timelock Security
boundary_details.description: Provides a delay for Guardian oversight before proposal execution

security_gap.gap_description: Wallet software may not sufficiently validate the authenticity or integrity of the provided RPC endpoint details.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard external user interacting with their local wallet software.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The behavior relies on the user obtaining correct network configuration details from potentially untrusted sources and inputting them accurately into their wallet.

assumptions: A malicious actor can provide a user with incorrect or malicious RPC endpoint details.
The wallet software does not have built-in mechanisms to verify the authenticity of the RPC endpoint or relies solely on user trust.
The user follows instructions from a malicious source (e.g., phishing website, fake social media post).

mitigation.strategy: Implement RPC endpoint verification mechanisms in wallet software and educate users on obtaining RPC details from trusted sources.

threats.description: An attacker can trick a user into adding a malicious RPC endpoint to their wallet when connecting to Berachain, allowing the attacker to potentially monitor transactions, return false data, or attempt to phish for signatures.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Vote selection (proposal ID, vote option) and user signature.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed if the sender's identity is not strongly verified.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with governance.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The contract may not adequately verify the signature linking the vote to the rightful BGT holder's key.

assumptions: The governance contract does not cryptographically verify the user's signature on the vote payload.
An attacker can craft transaction data appearing to originate from a different user's address without possessing their private key.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rigorous cryptographic signature verification for all vote submissions.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to submit fraudulent votes, potentially altering governance outcomes.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Vote input can be modified during transmission or processing.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with governance.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote data integrity may not be fully protected during submission or adequately validated by the contract.

assumptions: The channel used to submit votes is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks that allow data modification.
The governance contract does not check for vote payload integrity or consistency.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure vote submission uses secure, integrity-protected channels (e.g., HTTPS for RPC) and that the contract validates vote data.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts and alters a legitimate user's vote data during submission, changing their intended vote.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: A user can deny submitting a vote if no verifiable proof of origin exists.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with governance.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The contract or process does not require or record a cryptographically binding signature for each vote submission.

assumptions: Vote submission does not require a cryptographic signature.
Even if a signature is provided, it is not linked and stored immutably with the vote record.

mitigation.strategy: Require all vote submissions to be cryptographically signed by the user's private key and store this signature on-chain.

threats.description: A user submits a vote and later falsely claims they did not, undermining the integrity of the voting record.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Vote selection (proposal ID, vote option) and user signature.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Excessive vote submissions or malformed requests can make the voting service unavailable or degrade performance.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with governance.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The contract or underlying infrastructure may be vulnerable to resource exhaustion from a high volume of malicious vote transactions.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough that a large number of malicious transactions is economically feasible for an attacker.
The governance contract's voting logic is computationally expensive or vulnerable to state bloat under high load.

mitigation.strategy: Rely on blockchain transaction fees as a natural deterrent; optimize contract code for efficient processing; potentially implement application-level rate limiting if necessary.

threats.description: An attacker floods the governance contract with a large volume of valid or malformed vote transactions, preventing or delaying legitimate users from voting.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Vote selection (proposal ID, vote option) and user signature.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: An attacker can vote or influence governance outcomes without meeting the required BGT holding threshold or respecting vote weighting.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with governance.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The governance contract's logic for checking BGT balance/delegation or for calculating/counting votes is flawed.

assumptions: The governance contract incorrectly checks the user's BGT balance or delegation amount required for voting.
The vote counting mechanism is flawed, allowing double-counting or misapplication of vote weight based on BGT holdings.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust, audited logic for checking BGT balance/delegation at the specified snapshot block and for accurately counting votes.

threats.description: An attacker bypasses the BGT holding requirement or manipulates the system to cast more votes than entitled, gaining undue influence over governance.

attack_surface.data_description: $BGT delegation parameters (validator address, amount)

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of strong client-side validation and external user reliance on UI integrity.

actor_risk.description: The actor interacts via potentially untrusted interfaces.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Social engineering or UI compromise can trick users into delegating assets to malicious entities.

assumptions: An attacker can host a fake BeraHub interface or malicious website.
An attacker can social engineer a user into visiting the fake interface or signing a malicious transaction.
The user relies solely on the displayed information in the interface without external verification.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users about verifying URLs and using official interfaces; implement strong identity verification features in official UIs.

threats.description: An attacker spoofs the BeraHub interface or uses social engineering to trick a user into delegating their $BGT to a malicious address impersonating a legitimate validator.

trace_source.matched_capability: How To Boost A Validator With $BGT

attack_surface.data_description: $BGT delegation transaction payload before signing

security_gap.gap_description: The transaction payload can be maliciously modified on the user's endpoint before being signed.

actor_risk.description: The actor's local environment is a potential point of compromise.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malware on the user's endpoint can alter transaction details prior to cryptographic signing.

assumptions: The user's device is compromised by malware.
The malware can intercept and modify data between the DApp/Wallet and the signing process.
The user does not meticulously verify the full transaction details presented by their wallet.

mitigation.strategy: Promote endpoint security best practices for users; wallets should clearly display transaction details for user verification.

threats.description: Malware on a user's device tampers with the validator address or amount of $BGT in a delegation transaction before the user signs it, redirecting assets.

trace_source.matched_capability: How To Boost A Validator With $BGT

attack_surface.data_description: Signed $BGT delegation transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data is publicly readable on the blockchain.

actor_risk.description: The actor's activity and holdings related to boosting are exposed publicly.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Public transaction data is an inherent property of transparent blockchains, but may be a privacy concern for users.

assumptions: The blockchain is public and explorers allow viewing transaction details.
Users may have expectations of privacy regarding their staking/delegation strategies.
Wallet addresses can potentially be linked to real-world identities over time.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on blockchain transparency; future protocol enhancements could explore privacy-preserving delegation methods.

threats.description: Details of a user's $BGT delegation, including the amount and target validator, are recorded on the public blockchain and accessible to anyone.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction volume and gas usage for boost operations

security_gap.gap_description: Network congestion or transaction spam can prevent boost transactions from being processed or make them prohibitively expensive.

actor_risk.description: The actor's ability to participate is dependent on network availability and transaction costs.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The behavior relies on network transaction processing, which can be affected by congestion or attack.

assumptions: The blockchain network has finite transaction throughput capacity.
A malicious actor or high organic demand can fill block space.
Rising transaction fees can price users out of performing the action.

mitigation.strategy: Monitor network load; implement transaction prioritization mechanisms; optimize smart contract gas usage for boost operations.

threats.description: A network-wide DoS attack (e.g., transaction spam) or high network congestion prevents users from successfully queuing or activating their $BGT boosts.

attack_surface.data_description: Boost activation transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Flawed access control in the smart contract allows an unauthorized user to activate another user's queued boost.

actor_risk.description: The actor's queued boost could potentially be activated or manipulated by another malicious user.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting smart contract access control could allow hijacking user delegations.

assumptions: There is a vulnerability in the smart contract handling boost activation.
The vulnerability allows an address other than the one that queued the boost to trigger the activation function for that specific boost.
The attacker knows the details of a queued boost they want to hijack.

mitigation.strategy: Strictly enforce that only the address that initiated the 'Queue Boost' transaction can successfully execute the 'Activate Boost' transaction for that specific boost.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a smart contract vulnerability during the two-step boost process to activate a boost queued by a legitimate user, potentially gaining control over that delegation or associated rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

threats.description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

actor_risk.description: The actor interacts via an untrusted channel or client.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Standard web3 risk if user's client-side environment or network connection is compromised.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept/modify data between user client and transaction signing.
User client/environment is compromised.
Transaction signing is not end-to-end secure against client-side compromise.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure secure client-side transaction preparation and signing, educate users on security hygiene.

threats.description: An attacker could tamper with swap parameters (amount, asset pair) in a user's transaction before it is broadcast or signed.

security_gap.gap_description: RPC communication is unencrypted or intercepted, potentially revealing user data.

actor_risk.description: The actor connects via an untrusted network or uses a compromised RPC endpoint.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exposure of private keys is high risk, but typical RPC snooping reveals public addresses, balances, transaction history which is medium risk.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept network traffic to the RPC endpoint.
RPC endpoint is publicly accessible and/or unencrypted.
User is using an untrusted network connection.

mitigation.strategy: Use secure/trusted RPC endpoints (e.g., HTTPS, dedicated providers), use VPNs.

threats.description: An attacker could eavesdrop on the user's RPC communication to gather information about their wallet activities and balances.

trace_source.matched_capability: Copy and paste these values into any wallet that supports importing RPCs.

component_details.name: BeaconKit
component_details.description: Modular consensus layer framework integrating Execution Client with CometBFT.

boundary_details.name: Bridge Trust Boundary

security_gap.gap_description: Network or specific contract gas limits can be saturated by malicious transactions.

actor_risk.description: The actor has the ability to submit transactions, consuming network resources.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High transaction throughput on EVM chains can be challenging to secure against spam. Gas fees mitigate but don't eliminate the risk.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient BERA to pay for gas.
Network transaction processing capacity is limited.
RPC node capacity is limited.

mitigation.strategy: Implement effective gas pricing, transaction queuing, and RPC rate limiting.

threats.description: An attacker could flood the network with spam transactions (e.g., many small swaps) to increase gas prices or prevent legitimate transactions from being processed.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof validation logic is flawed, allowing unauthorized claims.

actor_risk.description: The actor interacts with the claiming mechanism.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised or flawed proof generation/validation allows attackers to steal incentives.

assumptions: Attacker can generate a valid-looking but fraudulent claim proof.
Proof generation system (backend API) is compromised or has a vulnerability.
Claim contract verification logic is incorrect.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure robust, securely generated, and correctly validated cryptographic proofs for claims.

threats.description: An attacker could attempt to forge the cryptographic proof required to claim incentives, thereby stealing rewards from legitimate users.

trace_source.matched_capability: claim their incentive token rewards through the [BGTIncentiveDistributor]

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: System relies on off-chain confirmation or lacks robust on-chain logging/proof of initiation.

actor_risk.description: The actor performs on-chain transactions.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are inherently non-repudiable due to signatures and on-chain immutability. Risk is minimal for core on-chain actions.

assumptions: System has a flaw where on-chain transaction finality or logging is not sufficient to prove intent or initiation.
Off-chain components involved in the process could be manipulated to support repudiation claims.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical actions rely solely on on-chain transaction finality and state changes.

threats.description: A user might attempt to deny having initiated a HONEY minting transaction, though standard blockchain properties make this difficult for on-chain actions.

trace_source.matched_capability: $HONEY can be minted by depositing whitelisted collateral

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of robust verification of vote origin against claimed voting power.

actor_risk.description: Standard external user interaction point for governance activities requiring BGT.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authentication or signature validation could allow widespread vote spoofing, undermining governance integrity.

assumptions: The system does not sufficiently verify the identity or authorization of the sender against their claimed voting power.
An attacker can craft and submit vote messages claiming arbitrary addresses as senders.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong cryptographic signature verification for all vote submissions, verifying the signature against the address holding or delegating the claimed voting power.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates legitimate BGT holders to submit fraudulent votes, distorting governance outcomes by voting with unauthorized power.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Vote message payload (proposal ID, vote choice, amount, delegation).

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Vote message content is not protected from modification in transit.

actor_risk.description: User client sends vote message across a potentially untrusted channel (e.g., internet).

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modification of votes could alter specific outcomes or invalidate legitimate votes, impacting governance results.

assumptions: The digital signature on the vote message only covers the origin/sender identity, not the entire vote payload.
An attacker can intercept and alter vote messages between the user client and the network/contract.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure cryptographic signatures cover the entire vote message payload, providing integrity protection.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts and modifies the content of a legitimate user's vote (e.g., changing their vote selection or delegated power) before it is processed by the governance contract.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Record of vote submission transaction.
