behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of irrefutable proof linking the user's off-chain action to the on-chain vote submission.

actor_risk.description: User interacting via potentially mixed off-chain (e.g., BeraHub interface) and on-chain methods.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions provide strong non-repudiation, but potential issues could arise with off-chain interfaces or weak proof of action.

assumptions: Votes submitted via off-chain interfaces (like BeraHub) are not sufficiently linked to the user's identity or signed in a way that prevents denial.
The on-chain transaction record's link to the user's intended action could be disputed.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all votes are recorded as cryptographically signed on-chain transactions directly attributable to the user's address.

threats.description: A user who submits a vote, particularly potentially via an off-chain interface like BeraHub, later denies having cast the vote.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: On-chain transaction data for votes, contract state storing vote data, linked off-chain profiles.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Sensitive vote data or links to voter identity are exposed publicly or to unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: User whose voting activity, voting power details, or linked identity is potentially exposed.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain data is public by nature; risk is primarily related to linking public vote data to private off-chain identities.

assumptions: Off-chain identity information is linked to on-chain voting addresses.
Publishing vote decisions and amounts on-chain reveals strategic or private information about large holders.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on best practices for privacy, avoid linking off-chain user identities directly to on-chain voting addresses.

threats.description: Sensitive information about user voting behavior, potentially linked to their identity or specific strategies, is publicly exposed via the blockchain or associated interfaces.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Network transaction processing capacity, contract gas limits, voting period end time, transaction fees.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: System resources (network bandwidth, block space, contract processing) can be exhausted during the voting period.

actor_risk.description: Any user can submit transactions, potentially spamming the network to disrupt availability.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Ability to disrupt a critical governance process by preventing legitimate votes from being recorded on-chain.

assumptions: Network transaction capacity can be overwhelmed by spam, making it difficult or expensive to submit votes.
Voting period is a hard deadline, making last-minute voting vulnerable to network congestion or attack.
Contract processing of votes has performance bottlenecks or gas limit vulnerabilities.

mitigation.strategy: Monitor network traffic and transaction patterns, optimize contract gas efficiency, ensure network robustness, potentially allow voting period extensions under specific, pre-defined conditions.

threats.description: An attacker floods the network or targets the governance contract with excessive transactions to prevent legitimate users from submitting their votes before the end of the voting period.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Manages the governance process, proposals, voting, and execution.

attack_surface.data_description: Contract logic for vote weight calculation, delegation mechanisms, proposal execution mechanism.

behavior_details.name: Provide Liquidity (BEX)
behavior_details.description: User deposits tokens into BEX liquidity pool

data_flow_details.description: User provides proof to claim incentives/fees

boundary_details.name: Berachain Network Perimeter
boundary_details.description: Protects the core blockchain network from external interactions

security_gap.gap_description: Flaws in privilege checks or vote weight calculation allow unauthorized influence over governance decisions.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to exploit contract vulnerabilities to gain undue voting power or control over the governance process.

actor_details.name: User
actor_details.description: General external user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting vulnerabilities in vote calculation or execution could allow an attacker to take control of critical governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract contains logic errors in calculating voting power based on BGT holdings or delegations.
A vulnerability exists in the delegation mechanism allowing unauthorized delegation or revocation.
An attacker can interfere with the proposal execution process via contract interaction or guardian manipulation.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct extensive auditing and formal verification of the governance contract logic, focusing on vote calculation, delegation, and execution mechanisms.

threats.description: An attacker exploits vulnerabilities in the governance contract to gain more voting power than their BGT holding or delegation entitles them to, or interferes with the execution of passed proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: vote selection and voter identity/delegation

security_gap.gap_description: Vote inputs may be spoofed without strong identity verification or signature checks.

actor_risk.description: User interacting with governance.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authentication allows impersonation for critical governance actions.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce strong cryptographic signature verification for vote messages.
Attacker can forge or replay vote messages.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all vote submissions.

threats.description: An attacker may impersonate a legitimate user or BGT holder to cast unauthorized or fraudulent votes on governance proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: vote selection and weight

security_gap.gap_description: Vote messages lack sufficient integrity checks before processing.

actor_risk.description: User sending vote message.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote integrity relies solely on standard blockchain transaction integrity; application-level checks may be missing.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept and modify the vote transaction before it is included in a block.
The smart contract does not sufficiently validate the integrity of the submitted vote data.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure the entire vote payload is cryptographically signed and verified by the smart contract.

threats.description: An attacker may tamper with the contents of a vote message (e.g., changing the vote selection or intended weight) before it is processed by the governance contract.

attack_surface.data_description: High volume of vote submissions or malformed vote messages

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract or underlying blockchain is vulnerable to transaction flooding or resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: User capable of submitting many transactions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Publicly accessible voting mechanism can be targeted by DoS attacks, potentially preventing legitimate participation.

assumptions: The blockchain network can be congested by a high volume of transactions.
The governance contract's gas limits or processing logic can be exploited to cause it to fail or reject valid votes under stress.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction prioritization based on fees and ensure robust smart contract gas handling; potentially require small staking for voting.

threats.description: An attacker may flood the network or target the governance contract with a large volume of transactions to prevent legitimate users from casting votes or disrupt the voting process.

attack_surface.data_description: vote message including claimed voting power

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract fails to correctly validate voter's BGT holdings/delegation or vote weight.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to gain unauthorized voting power.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting flaws in BGT balance checks or vote counting logic could grant unauthorized influence over governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract contains a bug in how it reads or validates the voter's BGT balance or delegated voting power.
The calculation or summation of vote weights is exploitable.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorous smart contract auditing and formal verification for BGT balance checks and vote counting logic.

threats.description: An attacker may exploit vulnerabilities in the governance contract to cast votes with more weight than their BGT holdings/delegation allows, or influence the vote count unfairly.

attack_surface.data_description: Voter identity and vote selection made public on-chain.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote details (voter, choice) are immediately publicly visible on the blockchain upon submission.

actor_risk.description: Source of the public vote data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Transparency of public blockchains makes vote details immediately visible, potentially impacting the voting process.

assumptions: The governance system does not implement a private voting mechanism or a commit-reveal scheme.
Real-time visibility of individual votes is considered a privacy or manipulation risk.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a commit-reveal voting scheme or explore zero-knowledge proof based private voting if vote privacy is a requirement.

threats.description: Due to the transparent nature of the blockchain, a user's vote and identity (associated with their BGT address) are publicly visible immediately after the vote is cast, which could potentially influence other voters or expose voter preferences.

attack_surface.data_description: vote selection (e.g., for, against)

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of robust identity verification and signature checking for vote submissions.

actor_risk.description: The actor is an external user interacting with the governance contract, potentially without strong multi-factor authentication.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized votes could significantly impact governance outcomes.

assumptions: The voting process doesn't strongly verify the voter's identity beyond the claimed address.
Signature verification of the vote message is missing or bypassable.

mitigation.strategy: Require cryptographic signature verification of vote messages linked to the BGT-holding address.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate BGT holder to cast unauthorized votes on governance proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: vote selection and associated parameters

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of integrity verification for vote message content before processing by the contract.

actor_risk.description: The actor is interacting over a potentially untrusted channel.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modified votes could skew governance outcomes.

assumptions: The data flow carrying the vote message lacks sufficient integrity protection during transit.
The contract doesn't verify the integrity of the vote message content upon receipt.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signing of the content of the vote message, verified by the contract.

threats.description: An attacker modifies a legitimate user's intended vote (e.g., changing 'for' to 'against') during transmission to the governance contract.

attack_surface.data_description: transaction volume for vote submissions

security_gap.gap_description: Potential susceptibility of the voting mechanism to transaction spam or resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: The actor can submit transactions that consume network and contract resources.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disruption of voting can prevent proposals from passing or failing, stalling governance.

assumptions: An attacker has enough resources (BERA for gas) to execute a transaction spam attack.
The contract or network infrastructure is susceptible to resource exhaustion from voting activity.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure efficient contract logic, rely on blockchain gas fees as an anti-DoS measure, and monitor for anomalous transaction patterns.

threats.description: An attacker floods the network or governance contract with numerous vote transactions, potentially disrupting legitimate voting or delaying proposal finalization.

attack_surface.data_description: vote submission transaction with parameters

security_gap.gap_description: Flawed authorization logic within the governance contract allows unauthorized or over-privileged voting.

actor_risk.description: The actor is attempting to perform a privileged action (influencing governance outcomes based on BGT holdings).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized privilege elevation in voting can lead to illegitimate governance decisions.

assumptions: The smart contract code for vote processing contains vulnerabilities in its BGT balance, delegation, or weight calculation checks.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct rigorous smart contract audits focusing on authorization and vote weight calculation logic.

threats.description: An attacker exploits vulnerabilities in the governance contract's authorization logic to cast votes without sufficient delegated BGT or gain disproportionate voting power.

attack_surface.data_description: proposal data (proposed changes, description, etc.)

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of robust identity verification and signature checking for proposal submissions from authorized addresses.

actor_risk.description: The actor is an external user interacting with the governance contract to initiate a significant process (proposal creation).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious or fraudulent proposals could lead to harmful governance decisions if they proceed through the process.

assumptions: The proposal creation process doesn't strongly verify the creator's identity beyond the claimed address with the minimum BGT.
Signature verification of the proposal data is missing or bypassable.

mitigation.strategy: Require cryptographic signature verification of proposal data linked to the authorized BGT-holding address.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate BGT holder with the minimum required BGT to create a fraudulent or malicious governance proposal.

attack_surface.data_description: proposal data including proposed actions/parameters

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of integrity verification for proposal data content before processing by the contract.

actor_risk.description: The actor is interacting over a potentially untrusted channel when submitting sensitive proposal data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modified proposal data could lead to unintended or malicious governance changes.

assumptions: The data flow carrying the proposal data lacks sufficient integrity protection during transit.
The contract doesn't verify the integrity of the proposal data content upon receipt.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signing of the content of the proposal data, verified by the contract.

threats.description: An attacker modifies a legitimate user's proposed changes or parameters within the proposal data during transmission to the governance contract.

attack_surface.data_description: transaction volume for proposal submissions

security_gap.gap_description: Potential susceptibility of the proposal creation mechanism to transaction spam or resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: The actor can submit transactions that consume network and contract resources, even if proposal validation fails.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disruption of proposal creation can prevent important governance changes or updates.

assumptions: An attacker has enough resources (BERA for gas) to execute a transaction spam attack targeting proposal creation.
The contract's proposal creation logic is susceptible to resource exhaustion, even if proposals are invalid.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure efficient contract logic for proposal validation and creation; rely on blockchain gas fees as a primary anti-DoS measure.

threats.description: An attacker floods the governance contract with numerous proposal creation attempts, potentially hindering legitimate users from submitting proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: proposal submission transaction with prerequisites

security_gap.gap_description: Flawed authorization logic within the governance contract allows unauthorized proposal creation (e.g., without required BGT).

actor_risk.description: The actor is attempting to perform a privileged action (initiating a governance proposal requires a minimum BGT threshold).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized proposal creation can allow malicious actors to initiate harmful governance changes.

assumptions: The smart contract code for proposal creation contains vulnerabilities in its minimum BGT balance or other prerequisite checks.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct rigorous smart contract audits focusing on authorization logic for proposal creation.

threats.description: An attacker exploits vulnerabilities in the governance contract's authorization logic to create a proposal without holding the required minimum 10,000 BGT.

attack_surface.data_description: Signed $BGT delegation transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of robust key management or signing process allows forging transactions.

actor_risk.description: Standard user interacting with blockchain via wallet.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Threat relies on compromised user private key, a standard user risk.

assumptions: Actor's private key is compromised.
Attacker can sign transactions on behalf of the actor.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong key management practices for users.

threats.description: An attacker compromises a user's wallet and signs a transaction to delegate the user's $BGT to a validator controlled by the attacker.

attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction with off-chain proof

security_gap.gap_description: Weak authentication or flawed proof verification allows unauthorized claiming.

actor_risk.description: Standard user interacting with blockchain via wallet.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Threat relies on compromised user private key or potential flaws in the off-chain proof system/on-chain verification.

assumptions: Actor's private key is compromised.
Attacker can sign claim transactions.
The attacker can obtain or forge a valid claim proof.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong key management for users and robust cryptographic proof verification.

threats.description: An attacker compromises a user's wallet and/or exploits a flaw in the incentive proof system to claim the user's earned incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of robust key management allows forging claim transactions.

actor_risk.description: Standard user interacting with blockchain via wallet.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Threat relies on compromised user private key, a standard user risk.

assumptions: Actor's private key is compromised.
Attacker can sign claim transactions on behalf of the actor.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong key management practices for users.

threats.description: An attacker compromises a user's wallet and signs a transaction to claim the user's earned $BGT rewards from a Reward Vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain generated claim proof data/backend system

security_gap.gap_description: Off-chain system or proof format reveals private user incentive eligibility/amounts.

actor_risk.description: User whose private incentive data is stored or processed off-chain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Dependency on a potentially less transparent/auditable off-chain system for proof generation.

assumptions: The off-chain backend generating claim proofs is compromised.
Sensitive details about individual user incentive eligibility or amounts are stored or processed by the backend.

mitigation.strategy: Secure the off-chain backend system and minimize sensitive data stored or included in proofs.

threats.description: A compromise of the off-chain backend system used for generating incentive claim proofs leads to the unauthorized disclosure of sensitive user incentive eligibility data.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction processing on chain

security_gap.gap_description: Network or contract overload/vulnerability prevents boost transactions from being processed.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to perform a time-sensitive action on chain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Can be impacted by general network congestion or specific contract vulnerabilities.

assumptions: The network is experiencing severe congestion (e.g., transaction spam).
A vulnerability exists in the Validator Contract that can be exploited for DoS.

mitigation.strategy: Implement network-level DoS protections and rigorously audit smart contracts for vulnerabilities.

threats.description: An attacker causes network congestion or exploits a contract vulnerability, preventing users from delegating $BGT to validators, impacting their ability to earn future incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim processing on chain and off-chain proof generation

security_gap.gap_description: Failure of the off-chain proof system or contract vulnerability prevents claims.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to claim earned rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Relies on availability of the off-chain proof generation backend, creating a single point of failure for the claiming process.

assumptions: The off-chain backend responsible for generating claim proofs becomes unavailable or malfunctions.
A vulnerability exists in the Incentive Distributor Contract that can be exploited for DoS.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure high availability and resilience of the off-chain proof backend and rigorously audit the claim contract.

threats.description: An attacker disrupts the off-chain service generating incentive claim proofs or exploits a contract vulnerability, preventing users from claiming their earned incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction processing on chain

security_gap.gap_description: Network or contract overload/vulnerability prevents claim transactions from being processed.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to claim earned rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Can be impacted by general network congestion or specific contract vulnerabilities.

assumptions: The network is experiencing severe congestion (e.g., transaction spam).
A vulnerability exists in the Reward Vault Contract that can be exploited for DoS.

mitigation.strategy: Implement network-level DoS protections and rigorously audit smart contracts for vulnerabilities.

threats.description: An attacker causes network congestion or exploits a contract vulnerability, preventing users from claiming their earned $BGT rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Delegation request (user ID, validator ID, BGT amount, signature)

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation request may be spoofed without proper identity verification.

actor_risk.description: External user interacts with the delegation contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authentication on delegation requests could allow impersonation, leading to unauthorized BGT delegation.

assumptions: Attacker can obtain or guess another user's identity.
Delegation contract relies solely on user ID without strong cryptographic proof of identity/intent.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce cryptographic signatures for all delegation requests linked to the user's wallet address.

threats.description: An attacker may impersonate a user to delegate their BGT to a validator chosen by the attacker.

attack_surface.data_description: Delegation request data in transit

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation request data integrity is not guaranteed during transmission.

actor_risk.description: External user initiates data flow susceptible to tampering.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data flow integrity between user wallet and smart contract is critical for correct delegation.

assumptions: Communication channel between user and contract is compromised.
Signature does not cover the entire transaction payload.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure transaction signatures cover all critical delegation parameters including validator ID and BGT amount.

threats.description: An attacker may alter the validator ID or BGT amount in a delegation request during transmission to redirect delegation or change the amount.

attack_surface.data_description: Delegation action record on blockchain

security_gap.gap_description: User may deny initiating delegation.

actor_risk.description: External user performs an action they might later deny.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of cryptographically verifiable actions could lead to disputes over delegation history.

assumptions: User wallet does not sign transactions.
Transaction history is not persistently recorded in a non-repudiable manner.

mitigation.strategy: Require user's cryptographic signature for all delegation transactions and record them immutably on the blockchain.

threats.description: A user who delegated BGT to a validator may later deny having performed the delegation.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction volume directed at the delegation contract

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation contract or network can be overwhelmed by excessive transaction volume.

actor_risk.description: External user can send transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Overwhelming the delegation mechanism prevents legitimate users from participating in the PoL system by boosting validators.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough to be economically feasible for an attacker to send a large volume of transactions.
Contract or network infrastructure cannot handle high transaction volume directed at this specific behavior.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic transaction fees or rate limiting at the contract or network level; ensure robust infrastructure capable of handling expected load.

threats.description: An attacker floods the delegation contract or network with a high volume of transactions to prevent legitimate users from boosting validators.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request including user identifier and proof

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request may be spoofed using a compromised or forged proof.

actor_risk.description: External user interacts with the claim contract using a proof of entitlement.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak proof verification or identity binding allows attackers to claim others' incentives.

assumptions: Attacker obtains a legitimate user's claim proof.
Claim verification logic does not strongly bind the proof or claim to the transaction initiator's identity.

mitigation.strategy: Require cryptographic signatures for claim transactions, linking the claim request and proof to the user's wallet address.

threats.description: An attacker may impersonate a user by using their claim proof to steal their incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request data (user ID, proof, requested amount/tokens) in transit

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request data integrity is not guaranteed during transmission or processing.

actor_risk.description: External user initiates data flow susceptible to tampering.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data flow integrity is critical to prevent unauthorized claim modifications.

assumptions: Communication channel between user and contract is compromised.
Claim request or proof can be altered in transit or before verification.
Transaction signature does not cover all critical claim parameters.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure transaction signatures cover all critical claim parameters and that the claim proof itself is tamper-evident.

threats.description: An attacker may alter the recipient address, claimed amount, or proof data in a claim request during transmission.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim action record on blockchain

security_gap.gap_description: User may deny initiating claim.

actor_risk.description: External user performs an action they might later deny.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of cryptographically verifiable actions could lead to disputes over claim history, especially if a claim is performed fraudulently by a spoofed identity.

assumptions: User wallet does not sign transactions.
Transaction history is not persistently recorded in a non-repudiable manner.

mitigation.strategy: Require user's cryptographic signature for all claim transactions and record them immutably on the blockchain.

threats.description: A user who claimed incentives may later deny having performed the claim.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request/proof data; Data flow 7: Incentive Token Transfer data

security_gap.gap_description: Sensitive claim entitlement or proof-related information is disclosed.

actor_risk.description: External user initiating/receiving data that might contain sensitive info.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transaction data is public, but exposure of sensitive off-chain data used for proofs is a concern.

assumptions: Claim proofs or associated off-chain data contain sensitive user information not intended for public view.
Communication channels or off-chain storage for proofs are not adequately secured.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize sensitive data included in on-chain proofs; secure off-chain data storage and communication channels.

threats.description: Sensitive information about user entitlements, claim proofs, or claim activity is exposed to unauthorized parties.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction volume directed at the claim contract

security_gap.gap_description: Claim contract or network can be overwhelmed by excessive transaction volume.

actor_risk.description: External user can send transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Overwhelming the claim mechanism prevents legitimate users from accessing their earned incentives.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough to be economically feasible for an attacker to send a large volume of transactions.
Contract or network infrastructure cannot handle high transaction volume directed at this specific behavior.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic transaction fees or rate limiting at the contract or network level; ensure robust infrastructure capable of handling expected load.

threats.description: An attacker floods the claim contract or network with transactions to prevent legitimate users from claiming their incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request/proof validation logic

security_gap.gap_description: Attacker exploits vulnerability to claim unauthorized incentives.

actor_risk.description: External user interacts with a potentially vulnerable contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A flaw in claim logic or proof verification could lead to theft of user funds (incentives).

assumptions: Claim proof verification logic is flawed (e.g., susceptible to forged proofs or parameter manipulation).
Authorization checks within the claim contract are insufficient to prevent claiming more than entitled or claiming others' assets.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough security audits of the claim contract logic and proof verification mechanisms.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the claim contract or proof mechanism to claim incentives they are not entitled to, potentially including those belonging to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof (e.g., Merkle proof) and parameters (amount, user address)

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof is forged or inaccurate, allowing attacker to claim incentives for others.

actor_risk.description: An external user could potentially use a forged proof to claim entitlements they do not own.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to theft of incentive tokens from legitimate users.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract's proof verification is faulty or can be bypassed.
An attacker can obtain or generate a valid-looking forged proof for another user's claim.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust Merkle proof verification in the smart contract.

threats.description: An attacker spoofs a user's claim entitlement by providing a forged proof to the BGT Incentive Distributor contract, stealing incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction data (amount, recipient address, proof)

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Claim transaction parameters (e.g., amount) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could potentially alter claim details to steal more or redirect funds if the transaction is not properly signed and verified.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with claim transactions can lead to theft or incorrect distribution of assets.

assumptions: The claim transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with the claim transaction data to alter the amount claimed or redirect incentives to their own address.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction and proof

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows claiming more than entitled or claiming for others.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to claim assets they are not entitled to or claim assets belonging to others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws leads to unauthorized access and theft of assets.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract lacks sufficient checks on claim proof validity or claimed amounts.
The contract does not correctly enforce that the claimant is the rightful owner of the entitlement.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control and validation logic in the smart contract for claiming.

threats.description: An attacker elevates their privilege to claim incentives they are not entitled to or claim incentives belonging to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue Boost transaction details (validator, BGT amount, user address)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction is initiated without proper authentication/signature from the legitimate user.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to initiate queue boost transactions on behalf of others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofed transactions can lead to unauthorized manipulation of BGT boosting and validator influence.

assumptions: The transaction is not properly signed by the user's wallet.
The Governance Contract fails to verify the transaction sender against the intended user.

mitigation.strategy: Require standard EVM transaction signing for all actions and verify sender address in contract.

threats.description: An attacker spoofs a user's identity to queue a validator boost using their BGT without authorization.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue Boost transaction data (validator, BGT amount)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters (validator, amount) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to alter the details of a queue boost transaction initiated by someone else.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with queue boost transactions can lead to misallocation of BGT boosts.

assumptions: The queue boost transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with a user's queue boost transaction to change the target validator or the amount of BGT being queued.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue Boost transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows queuing boosts for others, without sufficient BGT, or bypassing limits.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to queue boosts for arbitrary addresses, with arbitrary amounts, or bypass contract-defined limits.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws allows manipulation of the BGT boosting mechanism.

assumptions: The Governance Contract lacks sufficient checks on the sender's identity or BGT balance when queuing boosts.
The contract does not enforce limits on the amount of BGT that can be queued.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control, balance checks, and limit enforcement in the smart contract for queuing boosts.

threats.description: An attacker elevates their privilege to queue validator boosts on behalf of other users, queue excessive amounts of BGT, or bypass other restrictions.

attack_surface.data_description: Activate Boost transaction details (user address, target validator, amount)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction is initiated without proper authentication/signature from the legitimate user.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to initiate activate boost transactions on behalf of others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofed activation leads to unauthorized manipulation of BGT boosting, potentially at an inopportune time for the user.

assumptions: The transaction is not properly signed by the user's wallet.
The Governance Contract fails to verify the transaction sender against the intended user.

mitigation.strategy: Require standard EVM transaction signing for all actions and verify sender address in contract.

threats.description: An attacker spoofs a user's identity to activate their queued validator boost without authorization.

attack_surface.data_description: Activate Boost transaction data (user address, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters (target validator) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to alter the details of an activate boost transaction initiated by someone else.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with activate boost transactions can lead to misallocation of BGT boosts.

assumptions: The activate boost transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with a user's activate boost transaction to change the target validator.

attack_surface.data_description: Activate Boost transaction

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows activating boosts for others or bypassing the queue time.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to activate queued boosts belonging to other users or activate boosts before the required queue time has elapsed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws allows manipulation of the BGT boosting process, affecting validator selection and user rewards.

assumptions: The Governance Contract lacks sufficient checks on the sender's identity when activating boosts.
The contract does not correctly enforce the queue time requirement before allowing activation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control and state checks (queue time elapsed) in the smart contract for activating boosts.

threats.description: An attacker elevates their privilege to activate queued validator boosts on behalf of other users or bypass the mandatory queueing period.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: The smart contract is susceptible to resource exhaustion from excessive malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: An external user can send excessive transactions to the contract, potentially consuming all available gas or exploiting expensive operations.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A DoS attack can prevent legitimate users from claiming their earned incentives.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract's claim function is computationally expensive or vulnerable to spam.
The blockchain network cannot handle a large volume of malicious transactions targeted at this contract.

mitigation.strategy: Optimize contract functions for gas efficiency; Implement state rent or other layer 1 anti-DoS measures.

threats.description: An attacker floods the BGT Incentive Distributor contract with transactions, causing denial of service and preventing users from claiming incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue/Activate Boost transactions

security_gap.gap_description: The smart contract is susceptible to resource exhaustion from excessive malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: An external user can send excessive transactions to the contract, potentially consuming all available gas or exploiting expensive operations.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A DoS attack can prevent legitimate users from queueing or activating boosts, disrupting the boosting mechanism.

assumptions: The Governance Contract's queue/activate functions are computationally expensive or vulnerable to spam.
The blockchain network cannot handle a large volume of malicious transactions targeted at this contract.

mitigation.strategy: Optimize contract functions for gas efficiency; Implement state rent or other layer 1 anti-DoS measures.

threats.description: An attacker floods the Governance Contract with queue/activate boost transactions, causing denial of service and preventing users from managing their boosts.

attack_surface.data_description: Frontend display data and user interaction prompts/feedback

security_gap.gap_description: Compromised frontend can alter displayed information or transaction details before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with frontend, trusting its integrity.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with the frontend can lead to phishing private keys or unauthorized transactions.

assumptions: Attacker can compromise the delivery mechanism of the BeraHub frontend.
User interacts with the compromised frontend.

mitigation.strategy: Implement Content Security Policy and Subresource Integrity, secure build and deployment pipeline.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with the BeraHub frontend to steal user's private key or trick the user into signing malicious transactions.

attack_surface.data_description: Frontend display data and user interaction prompts/feedback

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend inadvertently displays or logs sensitive user interaction data.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with frontend, potentially exposing sensitive data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Sensitive data exposure can lead to privacy violations or targeted attacks.

assumptions: BeraHub frontend or associated logging reveals sensitive user data.
Attacker can access these logs or intercept frontend communication.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize data displayed and logged on frontend, encrypt sensitive communication.

threats.description: BeraHub frontend or associated services disclose sensitive user interaction data through logs or unsecured channels.

attack_surface.data_description: User input for wallet connection

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend infrastructure is unable to handle high volume malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: User sends requests to the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Standard web infrastructure mitigates this common threat.

assumptions: BeraHub frontend lacks sufficient DoS protection mechanisms.

mitigation.strategy: Implement DDoS protection, rate limiting, and proper traffic filtering.

threats.description: An attacker floods the BeraHub frontend with excessive requests, causing it to become unavailable to legitimate users.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious frontend spoofs legitimate wallet connection requests to phish user credentials.

actor_risk.description: User initiates wallet connection, trusting the dApp interface.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to compromise of user's private key.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User's wallet software does not sufficiently warn about phishing attempts.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on wallet security, rely on wallet software warnings, use browser extensions for dApp verification.

threats.description: A malicious BeraHub frontend spoofs the wallet connection interface to trick the user into revealing their private key or seed phrase.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious frontend tampers with connection parameters or requested permissions without user's full awareness.

actor_risk.description: User initiates wallet connection and approves requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering can lead to granting excessive permissions or connecting to an unintended network.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User approves modified connection requests without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet software should clearly display requested permissions and connection details for user review.

threats.description: A malicious BeraHub frontend tampers with the wallet connection request to gain broader permissions or connect to a different chain than intended.

attack_surface.data_description: User wallet address and public key

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend or backend stores or processes excessive user wallet data upon connection.

actor_risk.description: User connects wallet, potentially exposing wallet details.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Excessive data collection poses a privacy risk to the user.

assumptions: BeraHub backend collects and stores more user wallet data than necessary.
Backend is vulnerable to data breaches.

mitigation.strategy: Implement data minimization policies, secure data storage, and access control on backend systems.

threats.description: BeraHub frontend or backend collects and potentially leaks sensitive user wallet data (e.g., transaction history, balances) beyond the wallet address upon connection.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious dApp requests overly broad or unnecessary permissions from the user's wallet.

actor_risk.description: User approves requested wallet permissions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Granting excessive permissions can lead to unauthorized control over user assets.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User does not understand the implications of the requested permissions and approves them.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet software should present clear, granular permission requests and warnings for risky permissions.

threats.description: A malicious dApp requests excessive permissions (e.g., unlimited token spending) from the user's wallet during the connection process.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows unauthorized modification of staked balances.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the staking contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering leads to loss or manipulation of staked user assets.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, access control) allowing state manipulation.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices, implement reentrancy guards.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to tamper with other users' staked PoL assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate staking or unstaking.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from depositing or withdrawing funds.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement robust error handling.

threats.description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Reward Vault Contract, preventing legitimate users from staking or unstaking assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized administrative actions or control over staked assets.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could lead to loss of all staked assets in the vault.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has access control vulnerabilities.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control mechanisms, follow principle of least privilege, conduct access control audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to gain unauthorized administrative control or control over other users' staked assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows claiming BGT rewards that a user is not entitled to.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the claim contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows theft of user assets (BGT).

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability in its reward calculation or claim entitlement logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust reward tracking and claim logic, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to claim BGT rewards that belong to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows unauthorized modification of user BGT reward balances.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the claim contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering leads to loss or manipulation of earned BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of reward balances.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to tamper with other users' accumulated BGT reward balances.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate BGT claims or gets stuck in a state preventing claims.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from accessing earned BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management, consider emergency pause.

threats.description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Reward Vault Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from claiming BGT rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over claiming BGT rewards.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could lead to unauthorized distribution or theft of BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to claim functions.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive functions, follow principle of least privilege, conduct audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to gain unauthorized control over claiming functions (e.g., claiming for others).

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract allows unauthorized users to spoof boost contributions or amounts.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the boosting contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing undermines the Proof-of-Liquidity boosting mechanism and fairness.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has a vulnerability in its boost tracking or verification logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust boost tracking and verification logic, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to spoof BGT boost contributions, unfairly influencing validator rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract allows unauthorized modification of user boost amounts or total boost metrics.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the boosting contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering undermines the core PoL mechanism by manipulating boost influence.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of boost balances or system metrics.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management and calculations.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to tamper with other users' BGT boost amounts or the total boost metrics.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate boosting or unboosting.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from participating in the boosting mechanism, impacting PoL.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management, consider emergency pause.

threats.description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Validator Staking Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from boosting or unboosting BGT.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over boosting-related functions or metrics.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could allow manipulation of the boosting mechanism, impacting validator rewards and PoL alignment.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to boost functions.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive functions, follow principle of least privilege, conduct audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to gain unauthorized control over boosting functions or metrics.

attack_surface.data_description: Cryptographic proof data validating user's incentive entitlement

security_gap.gap_description: BGT Incentive Distributor contract or Backend API does not sufficiently validate incentive claim proofs, allowing spoofing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows theft of user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: Backend API's proof generation is flawed or BGT Incentive Distributor contract's proof verification is vulnerable.
Attacker can craft valid-looking fraudulent proofs.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a robust, audited cryptographic proof system, ensure strict on-chain verification.

threats.description: An attacker spoofs incentive entitlement proof data from the Backend API to the BGT Incentive Distributor contract, claiming incentives they did not earn.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain data related to incentive entitlements and claim history

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API storing incentive entitlement data is vulnerable to unauthorized tampering.

actor_risk.description: User data is stored and processed by the backend API.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with entitlement data directly impacts user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: Backend API has vulnerabilities (e.g., injection, weak access control) allowing data modification.
Attacker can access and exploit these vulnerabilities.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure API development practices, robust access control, input validation, regular security audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Backend API to tamper with user incentive entitlement data, altering their claimable amount.

attack_surface.data_description: Request for off-chain incentive claim proof (e.g., user address, claim period)

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing proof generation or claiming.

actor_risk.description: User requests proof or claims incentives.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from accessing earned Incentive Tokens.

assumptions: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience.
Attacker has resources to conduct a DoS attack.

mitigation.strategy: Implement DoS protection, rate limiting for API endpoints, ensure contract resilience.

threats.description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract, preventing users from claiming their validator incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Cryptographic proof data validating user's incentive entitlement

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or proof mechanism leaks sensitive data about user entitlements or claim history.

actor_risk.description: User's incentive entitlement data is processed by the backend API.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exposure of claim history or entitlement details can be a privacy violation.

assumptions: Backend API response includes excessive or sensitive data.
Attacker can intercept API communication or access API logs.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize data in API responses, implement access control to API, encrypt sensitive data transmission.

threats.description: The Backend API or the proof generation process discloses sensitive user data related to validator incentives or claim history.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain data related to incentive entitlements and claim history

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized manipulation of incentive entitlements.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claim mechanism, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation allows manipulation of all user incentive entitlements.

assumptions: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract has access control vulnerabilities.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive API endpoints and contract functions, conduct audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract to gain unauthorized control over incentive entitlement data for any user.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract allows unauthorized users to spoof voting power or propose malicious actions.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing undermines decentralized governance and decision making.

assumptions: Governance Contract has a vulnerability in its voting power calculation, proposal submission, or verification logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust voting power calculation and verification, secure proposal submission checks, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to spoof voting power or submit fraudulent governance proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract allows unauthorized modification of vote counts, proposal details, or governance parameters.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering undermines democratic governance processes and outcomes.

assumptions: Governance Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of votes, proposals, or parameters.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management, implement multi-signature for critical actions.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to tamper with vote counts, proposal details, or governance parameters.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate voting, proposing, or proposal execution.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Crippling governance prevents necessary protocol updates and parameter changes.

assumptions: Governance Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug in its lifecycle management.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management for governance lifecycle, utilize Guardian oversight.

threats.description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Governance Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from participating in governance processes.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over critical governance functions or parameters.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation allows an attacker to take control of the entire governance process.

assumptions: Governance Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to sensitive functions (e.g., proposal execution, parameter changes).
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control, require multi-signature for critical actions (Guardian system), implement time-locks for proposal execution, conduct audits.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to gain unauthorized administrative control over governance processes or parameters.
